source_jobs.title: Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

We're building a new way to experience fashion: try on clothes with your AI likeness, discover looks from others and shop your favorites. We're a small team of creative builders in NYC with a rare combination of taste and deep AI expertise. Our CEO designed avatars for Apple Vision Pro, and our CTO built consumer AI at DeepMind. We've collaborated with brands like Peter Do and been featured in Vogue. Backed by the investors behind OpenAI, Cursor and SKIMS, we're positioned at the intersection of AI, technology and culture.




THE ROLE

We're looking for someone who cares deeply about how interactions feel. You'll help build our iOS app, crafting the gestures, transitions and details that elevate the Doji experience.

At Doji, we treat engineering as a craft. The best native experiences come from people who obsess over details. We build in UIKit because it gives us the control to get those details right, and we use tools like Claude Code to move fast. That said, no UIKit experience is required - if you have the taste and fundamentals, we'll help you learn it.

This is an in-person role from our office in NYC.



You might be a great fit if you...

 - Have a design eye. You notice when a swipe dismiss feels slightly off, when an animation curve is wrong, when a gesture doesn't feel quite right. And you can't let it go.

 - Care about craft over credentials. You'd rather iterate 10x on one interaction than ship something "good enough".

 - Have strong programming fundamentals. We care more about how you think than what languages you know. Swift/UIKit experience is a plus, same with any strongly typed language - but what matters is that you can pick up new things quickly.

 - Are self-driven and high-agency. Startup pace, founder-level ownership.

 - Have genuine interest in fashion, avatars, or creative expression.

 - Know how to use AI coding tools, and when not to.
   
   

Nice to Have…

 - Prior iOS or UIKit experience

 - Experience building fluid animations and custom transitions

 - Familiarity with gesture systems and touch mechanics

 - Background in fashion, consumer e-commerce, or well-designed consumer apps



Doji is building the future of how people discover and try on fashion. If you're obsessed with the details that make digital experiences feel delightful, we want to talk.

source_jobs.title: Founding iOS Engineer
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

We're reimagining fashion shopping with AI avatars. To do this we're developing cutting-edge diffusion models and designing novel, personalized interfaces.

We're a small team of creative builders in NYC with a rare combination of taste and deep AI expertise. Previously we've shipped consumer products to millions at Apple, DeepMind, Meta, and startups. Backed by the investors behind OpenAI, Cursor, and SKIMS, our company is positioned at the intersection of AI, technology, and culture.




THE ROLE

We're looking for an iOS Engineer to craft a category-defining avatar fashion shopping experience. This role is in-person from our office in NYC.




As Founding iOS Engineer you will…

 - Develop a native iOS app for our evolving product

 - Work with the founders to design and implement novel mobile experiences

 - Build and maintain high-quality, reusable, and testable code

 - Define mobile development processes - including planning, testing, releasing, and monitoring

 - Collaborate effectively in a shared codebase while upholding high coding standards

 - Optimize app performance and speed

 - Stay up-to-date with the latest iOS developments and best practices

 - Contribute to the overall product strategy and user experience

You’re a great fit if you…

 - Have built consumer apps at scale

 - Have strong fundamentals and experience with the modern iOS stack (Swift, SwiftUI, UIKit)

 - Take pride in crafting intuitive user experiences, polished UI and maintainable, high-quality code

 - Understand low-level intricacies (Swift 6.0 concurrency) and know how to profile and measure app performance and speed

 - Apply Apple’s design principles and interface guidelines

 - Work with Git and Git flow methodology

 - Evaluate different design patterns and tradeoffs, specifically MVVM

 - Are self-driven, high-agency, and can master new skills quickly and deeply

 - Thrive on rigorous, fast-paced teams (startups or big tech)

 - Have genuine interest in fashion, avatars, or creative expression

Bonus if you…

 - Have experience with WebSockets, HTTP, concurrency and multithreading

 - Have experience building complex, fluid UX animations in iOS

 - Have worked in fashion or consumer e-commerce

source_jobs.title: Social Media Intern
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

We're reimagining fashion shopping with AI avatars. To do this we're developing cutting-edge diffusion models and designing novel, personalized interfaces.

We're a small team of creative builders in NYC with a rare combination of taste and deep AI expertise. Previously we've shipped consumer products to millions at Apple, DeepMind, Meta, and startups. Backed by the investors behind OpenAI, Cursor, and SKIMS, our company is positioned at the intersection of AI, technology, and culture.




THE ROLE

We're looking for a sharp, resourceful Social Media Intern to work in-person from our office in Brooklyn, NY. You'll help build a UGC creator program from scratch — sourcing creators, writing briefs, tracking what performs, and doubling down on what works.

You should be someone who's chronically online, with natural instincts for what goes viral and why. You see doomscrolling TikTok and Instagram as productive, and can break down why a video worked in seconds. Must be passionate about fashion.



As Social Media Intern you will…

 - Build and run a UGC creator program in-house — sourcing, outreach, onboarding, and managing creators

 - Develop content briefs that are tight enough for creators to execute without extensive training

 - Track performance across platforms, identify what's working, and get the whole program iterating on it fast

 - Experiment constantly with hooks, formats, sounds, and angles to find what converts

 - Build genuine relationships with creators that go beyond transactional

 - Source and recommend creators, references, and accounts

 - Use AI tools to move faster across outreach, briefing, reporting, and workflows

 - ... possibly even create content yourself
   
   

You’re a great fit if you…

 - Understand what makes great content and why it performs well

 - Can scroll your FYP and spot a future top creator before others realize it

 - Are organized enough to manage many creators with minimal hand-holding

 - Are a self-starter who figures things out quickly without a playbook

 - Write clearly and have strong instincts for tone and voice

 - Are comfortable with data — you can pull numbers, spot trends, and use it to make decisions

 - Have strong taste and a genuine love for fashion

 - Have experience editing videos

 - Are fluent with AI tools in your daily workflow

 - Want to win and care deeply about the quality of your work



Bonus if you…

 - Have made content yourself and understand the creator side firsthand

 - Have run or contributed to a creator or ambassador program before

 - Have experience with performance tracking tools

 - Have startup or fashion industry experience

 - Aspire to be a founder one day

source_jobs.title: Research Engineer
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

We're reimagining fashion shopping with AI avatars. To do this we're developing cutting-edge diffusion models and designing novel, personalized interfaces.

We're a small team of creative builders in NYC with a rare combination of taste and deep AI expertise. Previously we've shipped consumer products to millions at Apple, DeepMind, Meta, and startups. Backed by the investors behind OpenAI, Cursor, and SKIMS, our company is positioned at the intersection of AI, technology, and culture.




THE ROLE

We're looking for Research Engineers to help us build the next generation of our avatar and virtual try-on models. This role is in-person from our office in NYC.



As Research Engineer you will…

 - Implement cutting-edge generative AI features for inclusion in our product

 - Build data pipelines to feed large-scale image and video datasets into model training

 - Enhance model quality through user feedback and preference-based tuning

 - Improve virtual try-on for diverse body types, clothing styles, and aesthetic treatments

 - Collaborate effectively in a shared codebase while upholding high coding standards

 - Develop state-of-the-art personalized try-on capabilities in video and 3D

 - Work directly with our founders to create industry-leading technology

You're a great fit if you…

 - Have hands-on experience training and fine-tuning diffusion models (MUST-HAVE requirement)

 - Are familiar with implementing and optimizing diffusion model training pipelines

 - Care deeply about craft and creating category-defining experiences

 - Have worked in rigorous, fast-paced environments (i.e. startups) or have been a very productive IC in academia or open-source

 - Are self-driven, high-agency, and can learn new things quickly and deeply

 - Have implemented ML papers from scratch in pytorch or jax

 - Get excited about new ML methods, and read papers for fun

 - Have instincts on how to get unstuck when things don't work

 - Have genuine interest in fashion, avatars, or creative expression

Bonus if you…

 - Have experience with virtual try on

 - Have built consumer products

source_jobs.title: AI Creative Associate
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

We're reimagining fashion shopping with AI avatars. To do this we're developing cutting-edge diffusion models and designing novel, personalized interfaces.

We're a small team of creative builders in NYC with a rare combination of taste and deep AI expertise. Previously we've shipped consumer products to millions at Apple, DeepMind, Meta, and startups. Backed by the investors behind OpenAI, Cursor, and SKIMS, our company is positioned at the intersection of AI, technology, and culture.




THE ROLE

We're looking for a detail-obsessed AI Creative Associate to work in-person from our office in Williamsburg, Brooklyn. Your taste will directly shape the quality of our AI models. You should have a genuinely great eye: you notice when the lighting is wrong, when a fabric drapes unnaturally or when something is almost right but not quite.

This is a paid, full-time summer position (part-time possible) with potential to extend longer term. Great opportunity for ambitious students and early-career creatives. No technical background or resume pedigree required.

 

As AI Creative Associate you will…

 - Help teach our AI models to be more tasteful

 - Use AI tools to generate and edit images to the highest standard

 - Write and refine prompts to hit a specific creative target output

 - Compare versions of our upcoming models and pick which look best

 - Pull visual references to inform creative direction

 - Build your own systems and workflows to work faster and smarter

 - Develop content briefs that are tight enough for creators to execute without extensive training

You’re a great fit if you…

 - Have great taste and can articulate why something works

 - Are obsessive about details, down to the pixel

 - Are organized and self-directed enough to run your own workstream

 - Work hard and take feedback without ego

 - Have already made AI tools part of how you get things done

 - Hold a high bar for excellence, in your creative work and in life

 - Take pride in making repetitive tasks more efficient

 - Are tapped into fashion culture

 - Learn new things fast
   
    

Bonus if you…

 - Have ever built a tool just for yourself to solve a problem in your own life

 - Have photo editing experience (Photoshop or similar)

 - Have a background or education in fashion, photography, or design

 - Are the friend everyone asks for moodboards and visual references

source_jobs.title: Marketing Lead
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

We're reimagining fashion shopping with AI avatars. To do this we're developing cutting-edge diffusion models and designing novel, personalized interfaces.

We're a small team of creative builders in NYC with a rare combination of taste and deep AI expertise. Previously we've shipped consumer products to millions at Apple, DeepMind, Meta, and startups. Backed by the investors behind OpenAI, Cursor, and SKIMS, our company is positioned at the intersection of AI, technology, and culture.




THE ROLE

We're looking for a Marketing Lead to drive user growth and build community. This role is in-person from our office in NYC.



As Marketing Lead you will…

 - Work directly with our founders and creative director to hit growth goals while maintaining a consistent brand voice

 - Craft native engagement strategies across TikTok, Instagram, Twitter and nascent channels

 - Conduct measured experiments to determine which content formats, hooks are most effective to drive growth

 - Manage our social media presence and content strategy

 - Amplify UGC and organic product-led growth to build community

You're a great fit if you…

 - Are equal parts creative and analytical

 - Can effectively acquire users with minimal marketing spend

 - Are chronically online and speak both internet and fashion culture fluently

 - Excel at cultivating and growing engaged communities

 - Have experience conducting experiments with and managing content creators

 - Have expertise growing social media accounts to massive scale

Bonus if you…

 - Have experience marketing fashion brands or consumer tech products

 - Have experience with influencer marketing

source_jobs.title: Founding Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Opportunity

We’re building the future of fashion shopping with AI avatars: personalized try-on, social discovery, and interfaces that make shopping feel alive.

We’re a small, NYC-based team reimagining fashion shopping from the ground up. Our vision is playful, deeply personal, and centered on self-expression. To bring it to life, we combine advanced AI research, meticulous attention to detail, and an intuitive grasp of contemporary culture.

Previously we’ve built products used by millions at Apple, DeepMind, Meta, Shopify, and startups. Now we’re backed by the investors behind OpenAI, Cursor, and SKIMS.

The Role

We’re looking for a founding engineer who can move fast across the stack and go deep when the problem demands it. Early on, the work will be varied: one week you might ship a scrappy 0-to-1 prototype, the next you might optimize a feed ranker, debug a slow endpoint, or build infrastructure the rest of the team depends on. Over time, as the company grows, you’ll own the areas where you’re strongest.

This is an in-person role from our office in NYC.

The best person for this role is T-shaped: broad enough to move across AI, backend, data, and product surfaces when needed, with real depth in at least one of these areas:

 - AI product systems - diffusion pipelines, batch LLM workflows, vector search, evals, structured outputs, and reliability work under real product constraints.

 - Recsys and personalization - retrieval and ranking systems, embedding-based search, learned rankers, sequence modeling of user behavior, and online experimentation.

 - Core product infrastructure - databases and query optimization, data warehousing, infra-as-code, deployment, observability. You know where latency, reliability, and cost problems usually hide.

As a Founding Engineer you may…

 - Build end-to-end prototypes fast

 - Go deep when necessary: optimize the feed ranker, profile a slow endpoint, debug a gnarly race condition, chase down a memory leak

 - Own personalization end-to-end: retrieval, ranking, cold-start, and the tradeoffs that make recommendations feel fresh and relevant

 - Ship infrastructure the team builds on top of: data pipelines, internal tools, eval systems

 - Build LLM-powered systems that actually work in production

 - Work directly with the founders to turn early product ideas into working software

 - Contribute to product strategy and figure out what's worth building

You're a great fit if you…

 - Can move between scrappy prototyping and deep technical work

 - Ramp fast on unfamiliar domains

 - Have taste for consumer products and opinions about what makes them good

 - Have worked on consumer products, social products, marketplaces, or other user-facing systems at meaningful scale

 - Are self-driven, high-agency, and good at getting unstuck

 - Have genuine interest in fashion, avatars, or creative expression

Bonus if you…

 - Have shipped iOS or other native mobile experiences

 - Have worked on the infrastructure side of AI/ML - training pipelines, eval systems, data flywheels

notes: AI-focused fashion startup with strong engineering core
